DISCOVERY, 302, PLAINTIFFS CARLOS ROSADO AND LISSETTE OSORIO'S MOTION FOR ORDER PERMITTING DISCOVERY OF FINANCIAL CONDITION IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS' RIGHT OF FINANCIAL PRIVACY IS CONSTITUTIONALLY PROTECTED. VALLEY BANK OF NEVADA VS. SUPERIOR COURT (1975) 15 C3D 652. PRE-TRIAL FINANCIAL DISCOVERY REQUIRES A COURT FINDING OF A SUBSTANTIAL PROBABILITY - DEFINED AS "VERY LIKELY" OR "STRONG LIKELIHOOD" - THAT PUNITIVE DAMAGES WILL BE AWARDED. EVIDENCE ON BOTH SIDES IS WEIGHED BY THE COURT. JABRO V. SUPERIOR COURT (2002) 115 CAL. RPTR. 2D 843. PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE REGARDING THE UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTION AND RELATED EVENTS CANNOT BE CONSIDERED. SEE CIVIL CODE 47(B) AND KERNER V. SUPERIOR COURT OF LOS ANGELES COUNTY (2012) 206 CAL. APP. 4TH 84. THE HABITABILITY ALLEGATIONS ASSERTED BY PLAINTIFFS MAY SUPPORT A PUNITIVE DAMAGES AWARD AT TRIAL. BUT AS PRESENTED AND DISPUTED IN THE MOTION, THE COURT DOES NOT FIND THE "SUBSTANTIAL PROBABILITY" SUFFICIENT TO DEFEAT DEFENDANTS' CONSTITUTIONAL PRIVACY PROTECTIONS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM: STEVEN STEIN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; NOT REPORTED; DEPT. 302.
